name: Harshkumar Patel
summary: I'm an AI Engineer who builds systems that don't just process data — they reason, remember, and act. From architecting Buddy, a fully offline cognitive AI with human-style memory, to winning Microsoft's Global Hackathon 2023 with a real-time speech system, I bring a rare combination of deep research knowledge and production engineering discipline. I specialize in LLM systems, multi-agent pipelines, and full-stack AI applications — building things that work in the real world, not just in notebooks.

description: Leveraged SQL and Python to extract, cleanse, and analyze large-scale operational datasets, identifying 5 critical KPIs that directly elevated strategic business alignment by 20%.
Boosted data processing time by 40% through the extensive use of Python data manipulation libraries (Pandas, NumPy) and highly optimized SQL queries on relational databases.
Boosted reporting efficiency by 25% by designing and automating end-to-end data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) pipelines, replacing manual reporting processes.
Developed 10+ interactive Tableau dashboards visualizing KPIs, revenue trends, inventory metrics, and financial performance, improving executive decision-making speed by 30%.
Performed root cause analysis on data quality issues, implementing validation checks and cleansing routines that significantly reduced downstream reporting errors.
Collaborated with operations, finance, and management teams to translate business questions into data queries and actionable analytical insights.
Built predictive models to forecast demand and inventory needs, enabling procurement teams to reduce overstock costs and improve supply chain efficiency.
Established standardized data documentation and reporting templates, reducing onboarding time for new analysts and improving cross-team data consistency.
Presented data findings and strategic recommendations to non-technical stakeholders through clear visualizations and concise executive summaries.
Supported ad-hoc analytical requests from leadership, consistently delivering accurate, timely insights that informed pricing, operations, and growth strategies.
Evaluated and improved AI-generated code responses across multiple programming languages and domains to systematically enhance model accuracy, reliability, and output quality.
Designed diverse coding prompts, edge-case test scenarios, and benchmark problem sets to train and stress-test AI coding assistants across varying difficulty levels.
Reviewed AI-generated outputs for correctness, time complexity, memory efficiency, and adherence to industry-standard programming best practices.
Provided detailed structured feedback and annotation rationales to guide model fine-tuning, resulting in measurable reductions in hallucinations and logic errors.
Contributed to human-in-the-loop (HITL) workflows critical to reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) pipelines used in modern LLM training.
Covered a broad range of topics including data structures, algorithms, REST API design, database queries, ML model implementations, and system design problems.
Spearheaded the end-to-end implementation of Speech Completion for Microsoft Teams Meetings and PowerPoint Presentations, enhancing productivity for millions of enterprise users.
Elevated real-time speech recognition accuracy by 80% through the design and integration of advanced neural network architectures optimized for low-latency inference.
Integrated GPT-4 API for intelligent outcome prediction and contextual speech completion, delivering a seamless user experience through a Gradio-based interactive UI.
Designed and implemented a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions, reducing deployment failures by 40% and cutting release cycle from 2 days to 1 day.
Collaborated closely with senior research scientists and cross-functional engineering teams to align the prototype with Microsoft's enterprise speech product roadmap.
Conducted rigorous performance benchmarking of speech models under real-world conditions, identifying bottlenecks and applying targeted optimizations.
Awarded Regional Winner at Microsoft Global Hackathon 2023 for developing a real-time speech summarization system that improved meeting accessibility and user productivity.
Leveraged SQL and Python to extract, cleanse, and analyze large-scale operational datasets, identifying 5 critical KPIs that elevated strategic business alignment by 20%.
Boosted data processing time by 40% through optimized Python data manipulation libraries and highly tuned SQL queries on relational databases.
Boosted reporting efficiency by 25% by designing and automating end-to-end ETL pipelines, replacing manual reporting processes.
Developed 10+ interactive Tableau dashboards visualizing KPIs, revenue trends, inventory metrics, and financial performance, improving executive decision-making speed by 30%.
Built predictive models to forecast demand and inventory needs, enabling procurement teams to reduce overstock costs and improve supply chain efficiency.
Presented data findings and strategic recommendations to non-technical stakeholders through clear visualizations and concise executive summaries.
Independently developed a Python-based costing system, reducing billing workload by 50% and cutting quotation turnaround time from 2 hours to 20 minutes.
Optimized SQL queries and applied efficient data structures to improve system reliability and response time by 60%.
Created automated SQL-based stock reports that cut reporting errors by 90%.
Coordinated with stakeholders to document SOPs and ensure transparency in production processes.
Delivered fixes under tight deadlines, demonstrating strong incident ownership and problem-solving skills.
Built 2 secure multi-user applications that increased document processing speed by 40% while meeting strict confidentiality requirements.
Developed modular codebases using design patterns (Singleton, Chain of Responsibility) and SOLID principles to promote maintainability.
Led technical design and implementation for two internal productivity tools while maintaining rigorous version control via Git.
Assisted in training and deploying multiple deep learning models across image classification and text processing tasks, improving classification accuracy from 70% to 92%.
Trained and evaluated CNN architectures for pattern detection and visual recognition tasks, iteratively improving model accuracy by 90% through architecture tuning and data augmentation.
Automated monitoring and alerting scripts for image and text processing pipelines, reducing system downtime by 25%.
Developed and maintained backend services using Flask, Django, and Jinja2, integrating trained models into web-facing applications for real-time inference.
Built responsive frontend interfaces using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to present model outputs to end users.
Participated actively in Agile sprint cycles including code reviews, bug triage, and retrospectives.

homepage.hero_description: I build intelligent systems that reason, remember, and act. From offline cognitive AI to multi-agent LLM pipelines — engineering AI that works in the real world.

career_highlights.detail: Built real-time speech completion for Teams & PowerPoint. Won Regional Hackathon 2023.
Evaluated and improved AI-generated code to train production LLMs via RLHF workflows.
Building RAG systems, agentic pipelines, and full-stack AI apps for clients globally.

projects.name: Buddy – Offline Cognitive AI
description: Designed and developed Buddy, a fully offline cognitive AI assistant capable of multi-step reasoning, long-term memory recall, and autonomous tool execution without any cloud dependency.

Built a multi-layer cognitive architecture (Memory Retrieval Gate → Brain → Planner → Tool Executor → Analyzer) enabling structured decision-making, goal decomposition, and intelligent task execution.
Implemented advanced human-style memory management across three tiers — flash, short-term, and long-term — using SQL for structured storage and Qdrant for semantic similarity-based retrieval.
Designed a memory consolidation engine that filters, merges, and promotes short-term interactions into structured long-term knowledge using vector similarity scoring and salience weighting.
Integrated local LLMs via llama.cpp with tuned inference parameters to achieve low-latency, deterministic, structured JSON outputs across multiple model backends.
Engineered a dynamic tool-calling framework supporting secure shell automation, file system operations, web search, and OS-aware command execution with sandboxed execution.
Developed a real-time speech interface using faster-whisper for continuous audio capture and transcription — hands-free interaction with no wake word required.
Applied advanced prompt and context engineering to maximize reasoning depth while minimizing token usage and inference latency on offline hardware.

projects.name: Auto-Resume Builder
description: Architected an end-to-end multi-agent pipeline comprising 7 specialized agents for automated, ATS-optimized resume generation — orchestrating scraping, semantic retrieval, rephrasing, scoring, and PDF rendering as independent stages.

Implemented a RAG system using ChromaDB and vector embeddings to semantically index master background data at bullet-point granularity, enabling high-precision experience retrieval against live job descriptions.
Engineered an NLP-driven ATS scoring agent that computes keyword match percentage between job descriptions and generated resume content, delivering actionable gap-analysis feedback before PDF output.

Integrated Google Gemini API across rephrasing and analysis agents to rewrite resume bullets with high-density keyword alignment, enforcing human-writing style constraints to minimize AI-detection risk.
Designed a dynamic PDF generation agent with automatic margin adjustment, font scaling, and line spacing optimization to guarantee consistent single-page compliance with US/Canadian resume standards.
Implemented a content filtering agent that selects and ranks the most semantically relevant experiences from the master background using cosine similarity scores against the target job description.

projects.name: JSON Repair Doctor
description: Designed a fast, deterministic JSON repair engine purpose-built for fixing malformed, AI-generated JSON produced by LLMs, agent planners, tool-calling pipelines, and automation workflows.
Engineered a robust syntax repair layer covering missing commas, missing colons, trailing commas, adjacent objects, semicolons, and inline comments — converting all to strict valid JSON.
Implemented comprehensive quote normalization: single-to-double quote conversion, smart quote replacement, unescaped inner quote resolution, broken escape repair, and unterminated string recovery.
Built schema-guided repair mode for semantic correction — enabling type coercion, missing field injection, and recursive subtree repair driven by a provided schema definition.
Architected with strict loop-termination guarantees and deterministic execution paths, eliminating infinite loop risks on deeply malformed inputs.
Designed for production safety with minimal data loss as a core constraint — never silently truncates, drops keys, or alters semantics beyond what is required for valid JSON output.

projects.name: Smart Product Pricing
description: Developed a multimodal deep learning pricing prediction system for a large-scale e-commerce dataset of 75,000 products, leveraging product text, images, and catalog attributes to generate optimal price recommendations.

Implemented OpenAI's CLIP model to extract rich cross-modal feature embeddings from product images and textual descriptions, enabling unified representation learning across heterogeneous catalog data.
Engineered end-to-end data preprocessing pipelines handling diverse inputs including raw catalog text, product imagery, and numerical pack quantity attributes at scale.
Improved model accuracy from a weak baseline to 92% on the held-out test set through systematic feature engineering, architecture selection, and hyperparameter tuning.
Generated and validated structured prediction outputs (CSV format) for all 75,000 test samples, demonstrating large-scale inference workflow management.
Benchmarked the CLIP-based multimodal approach against text-only and image-only baselines, quantifying the contribution of each modality to final pricing prediction performance.

projects.name: RSAD – AI Roadside Anomaly Detection
description: Designed and developed a full-stack AI-powered road traffic monitoring system capable of detecting multiple traffic violations in real time — wrong-way driving, helmet non-compliance, stop-line crossing, and traffic obstruction.

Trained and integrated a YOLOv3 object detection model to identify and localize vehicles and riders in live traffic feed frames, enabling frame-by-frame anomaly classification across four distinct violation categories.
Implemented automatic challan (traffic ticket) generation triggered on violation detection — extracting number plates via OpenCV, performing OCR with Tesseract, and linking to owner records in MySQL.
Applied OpenCV-based image processing including adaptive thresholding, Canny edge detection, and contour analysis to isolate and extract vehicle number plate regions for downstream OCR.
Built the backend using Flask with modular blueprint architecture, connecting the YOLOv3 inference engine, OpenCV pipeline, OCR module, and MySQL database into a cohesive application layer.
Developed role-based web portals for Admin and User access — Admins managing violation records and challan dispatch, Users viewing and responding to issued challans.

projects.name: Real-Time Speech Emotion Recognition
description: Trained a multiclass LSTM-based deep learning model to classify seven distinct emotions — Calm, Happy, Angry, Sad, Fearful, Disgust, and Neutral — from raw human speech signals, with an additional gender classification module.
Extracted multi-dimensional audio features per sample including RMSE, MFCCs, and Mel-Spectrograms using Librosa, providing the LSTM with rich acoustic representations.
Combined RAVDESS and TESS benchmark datasets for 8,480 labeled audio samples, applying data augmentation (noise injection, pitch shifting, time stretching) to improve generalization.
Achieved 99.06% training accuracy and 96.84% validation accuracy over 160 epochs with batch size 256, demonstrating strong generalization with minimal overfitting.
Engineered a real-time detection system using sounddevice for continuous microphone listening, segmenting the audio stream into 2.6-second clips with sub-second prediction latency.
Built a session summary module that aggregates per-clip emotion predictions to provide users with a statistical breakdown of emotional patterns detected over a full interaction.

projects.name: Real-Time Face Mask Detection
description: Developed a real-time face mask detection system using YOLOv5 capable of classifying three distinct states — Mask, No-Mask, and Improper-Mask — from live webcam feeds and pre-recorded video streams.
Trained a custom YOLOv5 model on a domain-specific dataset of 853 labeled images, achieving a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 0.85 across all three detection classes.
Applied data augmentation strategies including mosaic augmentation, random flipping, HSV color jitter, and scale variation to improve robustness across diverse lighting and face orientations.
Fine-tuned YOLOv5 hyperparameters including learning rate schedules, anchor sizes, and confidence thresholds to optimize precision-recall trade-off for the improper mask class.
Leveraged transfer learning from YOLOv5's COCO-pretrained weights to accelerate convergence on the small dataset, significantly reducing training time while achieving production-viable accuracy.
Engineered the detection pipeline to process both real-time webcam input and static video files, enabling flexible deployment across surveillance and compliance monitoring use cases.

projects.name: Cataract Eye Disease Detection
description: Designed and developed an AI diagnostic system for automated detection of cataract eye diseases from external eye images, targeting early detection to reduce dependency on specialist clinicians.

Implemented and benchmarked two CNN architectures — ResNet-50 and EfficientNet — for binary classification of normal vs. cataract-affected eyes, evaluating trade-offs in accuracy, speed, and generalization.
Applied transfer learning by fine-tuning pretrained ImageNet weights on domain-specific eye image data, accelerating convergence and improving performance on limited medical imaging samples.
Evaluated model performance using clinically relevant metrics including accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, precision, F1-score, and AUC-ROC.
Integrated the trained model into a user-friendly web application accepting uploaded eye images and returning real-time cataract diagnoses accessible to both medical professionals and patients.
Demonstrated potential for real-world healthcare impact in regions with limited access to ophthalmologists by providing a scalable, non-invasive, and cost-effective screening tool.

projects.name: Real-Time Gender Classifier by Voice
description: Developed a real-time voice-based gender classification system using LSTM neural networks trained on acoustic features extracted from audio signals.
Extracted acoustic features per audio sample using Librosa, including MFCCs, chroma features, and spectral contrast, providing the model with rich representations of vocal characteristics.
Implemented continuous real-time audio capture using sounddevice, processing incoming audio into fixed-length segments for live gender inference with low latency.
Applied data augmentation including pitch shifting and noise injection to improve model robustness across diverse speaker demographics, microphone types, and recording environments.
Trained and evaluated the LSTM model on benchmark voice datasets, achieving strong classification accuracy through systematic hyperparameter tuning and architecture optimization.
Structured the inference pipeline for easy integration into broader speech processing systems, with configurable confidence thresholds and real-time output rendering.

projects.name: 20 Newsgroup Text Classification
description: Built a multi-class text classification system on the 20 Newsgroups benchmark dataset, categorizing news articles across 20 distinct topic categories using classical and modern NLP techniques.
Applied comprehensive text preprocessing including tokenization, stop-word removal, stemming, and lemmatization using NLTK to clean and normalize raw newsgroup text data.
Engineered TF-IDF feature representations from preprocessed text, capturing term frequency and document relevance across the full 20-class vocabulary for downstream classification.
Trained and benchmarked multiple classification algorithms — Naive Bayes, SVM, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest — evaluating performance using accuracy, F1-score, and confusion matrices.
Conducted exploratory data analysis to identify class imbalances, vocabulary distributions, and cross-category topic overlap, informing preprocessing and model selection decisions.
Produced a comprehensive analysis report with visualizations documenting the methodology, experimental results, and comparative model performance across all 20 news categories.

projects.name: Python Costing & Billing System
description: Independently designed and developed a Python-based costing and billing system for G Das Industries in Uganda, reducing billing workload by 50% and cutting quotation turnaround time from 2 hours to 20 minutes.
Optimized SQL queries and applied efficient data structures to improve system reliability and response time by 60%, enabling smooth operation on constrained hardware.
Created automated SQL-based stock reporting that cut manual reporting errors by 90%, replacing error-prone spreadsheet workflows with reliable automated pipelines.
Coordinated directly with business stakeholders to gather requirements, document SOPs, and validate system outputs against real production data.
Delivered the full system under tight deadlines in a resource-constrained environment, demonstrating end-to-end ownership from requirements gathering through deployment.

projects.name: Chatbot Sarah
description: Designed and developed Sarah, a Python-based multimodal conversational AI supporting text chat, voice input via speech recognition, and voice output via text-to-speech synthesis.
Built a flexible intent classification engine trained on custom JSON-based training data (intents, patterns, responses, and context), enabling domain-specific conversational customization.
Implemented voice input processing using Python speech recognition libraries, converting live microphone audio to text and feeding it into the classification pipeline for hands-free interaction.
Engineered voice output using text-to-speech synthesis, allowing Sarah to respond audibly — supporting natural two-way spoken conversations without requiring a GUI.
Designed a configurable knowledge base architecture where conversation domains, response sets, and context rules are fully defined in external JSON files — enabling zero-code knowledge updates.
Structured the system with clean separation between the NLP classification engine, voice I/O layer, and knowledge base, allowing independent extension of each component.
